Q2 Not checked

Which of the following pairs of type of precipitation and its cause is incorrect?

Correct answer: B
Solution

Convective rainfall is due to rising warm air.

Q3 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A reservoir has a surface area of 1600 ha. In the month of May, average inflow rate is 9 m3/s, precipitation is 8 cm, average outflow is 14 m3/s and change in storage is 0.014 km3. Assuming surface losses to be 0.064 m, the evaporation in the month of May is ________ cm. (Round off to
one decimal place).

NAT
Correct answer: 5.4
Solution

5.4 (5.2 - 5.6)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A lined trapezoidal canal has the following characteristics:
• Full supply discharge = 200 cumec
• Side slope = 1.5 H : 1 V
• Bed slope = 1 in 5000
• Manning’s coefficient = 0.018
• Permissible velocity = 1.75 m/s
The depth (in meters, round off to three decimal places) of the water in canal is _____ .

NAT
Correct answer: 4.621
Solution

4.621 (4.615 - 4.625)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A storm lasting 3.5 hours produced the following rainfall intensities (cm/hr) for successive 30- minute periods : 6.0, 7.5, 11, 9, 4, 3.5, and 7. Assuming a constant of 4 cm/hr, the corresponding value of W-index (in cm/hr) will be:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 

 

Q6 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A stream of 5 m3/s of water is supplied to an agricultural field of 24 ha for a duration of 3 hours.
After irrigation, it is found that 0.21 m depth of water has actually been stored uniformly in the root zone. The water application efficiency for this system is ________ %. (Answer upto two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 93.33
Solution

93.33 (93.25 - 93.45)

 
 
Q7 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A loamy soil has a field capacity of 30% and a permanent wilting point of 12%. The root zone depth of a crop is 1.0 m and the specific gravity of the soil is 1.56. At a certain stage of growth, the average soil moisture content in the root zone is 18%. If the farmer irrigates the field by applying 28 cm of water then:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

In the context of precautions against failure of weir on permeable foundation, the correct statement(s) is/are:

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

Energy dissipator blocks are provided for energy dissipation and not for percolation control.

Q12 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the dam section shown below:

NAT
Correct answer: 383.66
Solution

383.66 (383 - 384)

 
Q13 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0

NAT
Correct answer: 11.65
Solution

11.65 (11.5 - 11.7)

Correct answer: B

A 30 solution of a fluorescent dye was discharged into a stream at a constant rate of 12 cm3/s.
The background concentration of the dye in the stream water was found to be zero. At a downstream section sufficiently far away, the dye was found to be in equilibrium concentration of 6 parts per billion. The discharge (in m3/s, round off to three decimal places) using constant
rate injection method is ______ .

NAT
Correct answer: 0.059
Solution

0.059 (0.05 - 0.07)

What will be the ratio of discharge per unit length in drain for areas A and B?
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Solution

Q23 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Interception losses in a hydrologic cycle include:

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Interception loss is the water which is captured by vegetation or other such type of obstruction
and which is subsequently evaporated.

Solution

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, c, d)

 
Q32 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A river basin is approximated by a semicircle of diameter 25 km. Above this diameter, an equilateral triangle having the diameter of semicircle as its base is also included as part of basin. Within this combined basin, five rain gauge stations (A, B, C, D and E) are established.
The coordinates of the stations (in km) with respect to an origin at the semicircle centre and xaxis along the diameter are : A(6, 6), B(–6, 6), C(–6, –6), D(6, –6) and E (0, 0). During a particular storm, the rainfall depths (in mm) recorded at these stations are : A(90), B(98), C(87), D(90) and E(109). Using Thiessen polygon method, the average rainfall depth (in mm, round off to two decimal places) over the entire basin is ____ .

NAT
Correct answer: 93.86
Solution

93.86 (93.5 - 94.5)
